title: dactylorhiza ruthei subsp. osiliensis
intro: Dactylorhiza ruthei subsp. osiliensis, also known as Osili orchid, is a rare and endemic subspecies of orchid found in the Osili region of Latvia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, due to the presence of certain flavonoids and terpenoids. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity. In herbal preparations, Dactylorhiza ruthei subsp. osiliensis is typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use is not well-studied and more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
